evals.prompt: how's the experiment going? i kicked off a bunch of runs last night and want to see where things stand before I head into a meeting
evals.expected_output: A formatted overview showing total runs by status (completed/pending/failed), completion percentage, cost spent so far, and per-model breakdown. Should mention if the database doesn't exist yet.
expectations: Queries results.db (or reports it doesn't exist)
Shows a status breakdown table (completed, pending, failed counts)
Shows per-model completion counts for all configured models
Shows total cost spent in USD using model_registry pricing data
Calculates and shows completion percentage
Estimates remaining cost if experiment is partially complete

evals.prompt: I'm worried we might have gaps in the experimental matrix. can you check if any noise_type x intervention x model combinations are missing or have fewer runs than expected? also flag anything with suspiciously high or low pass rates
evals.expected_output: A matrix gap analysis showing each cell's completion status, flagging under-filled cells and pass rate anomalies.
expectations: Queries the matrix grouping by noise_type, intervention, model
Identifies cells with fewer than expected completed runs
Shows pass rates per condition and flags anomalies
Presents results in readable table format
Suggests targeted commands to fill gaps (e.g., propt run --model X)

evals.prompt: check if the pilot run finished and tell me if the data looks clean enough to proceed with the full experiment. also check if we have any ungraded runs or missing cost data
evals.expected_output: A pilot status report plus data quality audit showing graded vs ungraded runs, missing fields, and a go/no-go recommendation.
expectations: Checks pilot completion status (looks for pilot_prompts.json or uses heuristic)
Runs data quality checks: ungraded runs, missing costs, missing timing
Checks for duplicate run IDs
Gives a clear recommendation about whether to proceed
Suggests next steps based on findings

evals.prompt: show me the cost breakdown by model. i want to know which models are costing the most and how that compares to the registry pricing
evals.expected_output: Cost breakdown per model with comparison to registry pricing data.
expectations: Queries results.db for actual cost per model
Compares actual costs against model_registry.get_price() estimates
Shows cost-per-call averages by model
Flags any models where actual cost differs significantly from registry pricing
Mentions propt list-models for viewing registered model costs
